title: Palala mexicana
taxonomy: Palala mexicana is classified within the infraorder Laniatores and the family Epedanidae. It belongs to the order Opiliones. The classification of Palala mexicana is further divided into the family level as Epedanidae, infraorder Laniatores, and order Opiliones. The infraorder Laniatores is a group of harvestmen, and they are characterized by having eyes arranged in two rows. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and diversity within the order Opiliones.
uses: Palala mexicana is a plant that has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various medicinal properties. The plant's extracts have been reported to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities.
